authors: Soulas T, Gurruchaga J-M, Palfi S, Cesaro P, Nguyen J-P, Fénelon G
title: Attempted and completed suicides after subthalamic nucleus stimulation for Parkinson's disease
study_question: Examine prevalence and characteristics of suicides after STN DBS for Parkinson's disease.
population_assessed: 200 Parkinson's disease patients (127 men, 73 women) after bilateral STN DBS.
follow_up_duration: Study period 1997-2006; suicidal events mean 12 months post-surgery.
outcome_measures: Completed suicide, attempted suicide, postoperative depression, impulse regulation.
inclusion_criteria: Parkinson's disease patients who underwent bilateral STN DBS in their center.
answer_to_study_question: Higher than expected suicide rate (1% completed, 2% attempted) after STN DBS, linked to depression/impulse dysregulation.
